notes.text: Create new projects, scan for existing ones, or import them directly
Download versions or scan for versions you already have
Categories and workspaces to keep your projects organized, whether they're personal, for game jams, or freelance work
Changelog view to track what's changed in each version of GodotHub
Settings view so you can tweak GodotHub to work the way you want
Dark and light mode with a customizable accent color
Pin projects to keep your most important ones at the top
Sort and filter your projects however you like
Search projects by name
Rename versions to use whatever naming style works for you
Auto bind projects to their matching Godot versions if you have them downloaded
Folder depth slider to control how deep the scan goes for projects and versions
Icon colors and hover states weren't updating when you changed the accent color in Settings, now they do
Uninstalling a version used to leave files behind, now it cleans everything up properly
Moved the Scan Now button to the top of the scan directories card in Settings so it's easier to find
Added Scan Now buttons directly in the Projects and Versions list views for quick manual rescans
Added a fallback download path for Godot versions in case you haven't set one up in Settings yet
Added a helpful description in Settings explaining the default download path for versions
Replaced the star system for setting default directories with checkboxes, it's clearer now
Drag and drop support for reordering projects in the list view using the dnd-kit library
Button in settings to wipe app data and start fresh, useful if something breaks or you just want a clean slate
Import versions the same way you import projects, with a dedicated import button
Categorization for versions in the Versions view
Filtering options for versions in the Versions view
Pause, resume, and cancel downloads for versions
Progress popup when scanning or importing
Categories collapse and expand in the Projects View
Right Click context menu for projects and versions with options to open, rename, delete, and more
Pin Projects to the sidebar for quick access anywhere in the app
'Auto-Scan on Startup' Settings option
If you minimize to system tray and try opening from the taskbar, it now brings back the existing instance instead of spawning a new one
Auto scan for projects and versions now runs right after onboarding
Fixed naming inconsistencies between imported and installed versions, they now show up the same way in the list
Imported versions were not being recognized as installed, so the Available versions list still showed Install instead of Installed, that's fixed now
If you delete an imported version from your file system, it now disappears from the list view automatically
Uninstalling an imported version now removes the console build along with it, no more leftover files
Download progress used to reset and show Install again when switching views, now it keeps your progress and shows the right state
Re-did the Settings Page layout
Tooltip UI for overall App.
Command Palette for Quick Access across the App.
Drag/Drop Import for Projects and Versions
Feeling lucky button to Appearance settings to randomly change the accent and background colors.. u might be feeling lucky?
Launch Arguments for Projects, Right Click Context menu to access it
Direct Project Cloning and Importing from Github
Git Integration with Commits History, Branches, Fetch, Pull, Commit, Push, and more
Report a bug section for bug reporting and feedback
Custom Icons when creating New Project
Light Mode accent and background colors can be changed now
Project Size Chart Modal that can be accessed via context menu right click on the Project Card
Removed Dragging from Pinned Projects and instead Arrows to move up and down
Recent Projects in the sidebar, Quantity can be controlled in settings
Search bar in settings
Export/Import option in Advanced menu of Settings
Changed Settings UI / UX
Launch Modal when launching a project
Custom Templates for your Project
Project Size Chart Modal that can be accessed via context menu right click on the Project Card
Auto Updater and Github Workflows to Auto Build for each OS
Auto updater wasn't working properly, now it checks for updates and downloads them correctly
UX Glitches and Improvements
